codeReview.summary: The field-type-aware RecordId compatibility seam is already present in the reviewed baseline: string-form compatibility now enters through deserialize_record_id_or_compat_string for RecordId-typed fields, while the generic decode_record_link_value path remains limited to explicit RecordId-style decoding. The commit delta from ef27504 to 3e529fa tightens global string classification and adds focused coverage without reintroducing ordinary-string coercion, so the feature requirements are met.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker skill assumes every assigned feature requires fresh failing-test-first implementation work, but this feature was already satisfied by the checked-out milestone baseline and the worker had to switch to verification-only mode. The skill should explicitly document this already-landed-baseline case so workers can follow procedure without looking non-compliant.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Worker handoff for session 268e1563-569b-45f2-b3b6-9dbf2d5b5d99 records followedProcedure=false solely because no edits were needed after verifying the baseline implementation at commit 3ff4d10.

summary: Pass. I reviewed the feature metadata, worker transcript skeleton, handoff, skill guidance, and the relevant commit history around ef27504 -> 3e529fa -> 3ff4d10. The code keeps arbitrary-table string-form RecordId compatibility on RecordId-typed deserialization while leaving ordinary String payloads untouched, and I found no blocking or non-blocking implementation issues for this feature.
